sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on the blue block at the end of the top assembly had no impact (no_impact: true). It just caused flash animations on all 9 blocks in the grid.
So far, the only action that actually changes the board state is clicking on white blocks in the bottom-right grid, which toggles them white↔green.
Let me reconsider the puzzle. Maybe the goal is simply to change all white blocks to green? The purple dots in the bottom-left container form the same cross pattern as the white blocks, suggesting the white blocks are the "target" blocks to change.
Let me try clicking on all 4 white blocks to turn them green. The white blocks are at:
- (49, 29) - top middle
- (54, 24) - middle left
- (54, 34) - middle right
- (59, 29) - bottom middle
I already toggled (49, 29) twice (white→green→white), so it's back to white. Let me click all 4 white blocks to turn them green.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
**World model:** Clicking the top assembly had no impact. Only clicking white blocks in the bottom-right grid changes the board (white↔green toggle). The purple dots in the bottom-left form the same cross pattern as the white blocks.
**Goal model:** Likely to change all white blocks to green (matching the pattern indicated by purple dots).
**Action model:** Clicking white blocks toggles them to green. Need to click all 4 white blocks.
**Plan:** Click all 4 remaining white blocks to turn them green. The white blocks are at positions (49,29), (54,24), (54,34), (59,29).

    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 35},  # middle right

])
</parameter>
